channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Takeaways from the Gene Hackman and Betsy Arakawa deaths investigation
item.description: Authorities say Gene Hackman spent his final years retired from acting, living with heart disease and Alzheimer’s.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Venezuelan parents watch from home as their son is laid to rest in Texas after drowning
item.description: The cries of a mother watching from Venezuela through a cellphone pierced through the somber Christian music and gusts of wind as her son's casket was lowered into the ground.
item.dc:creator: Valerie Gonzalez, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Hawks, butterflies and a song for her murdered son help Georgia mom reclaim joy after killings
item.description: ROSWELL, Ga. (AP) — A hawk swooped in and landed on a tree, then watched as Michele and Jeremy Davis mourned their murdered son at a vigil outside his high school.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Alabama minor is jailed with adults pre-trial in violation of federal law
item.description: MONTGOMERY, Ala. (AP) — Djovani Jean-Pierre spent his 17th birthday in a small county jail in rural northern Alabama, where he has languished for more than half the school year awaiting trial. On the advice of his 51-year-old cellmate, Jean-Pierre avoids contact with others in the jail.
item.dc:creator: SAFIYAH RIDDLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Violent and sudden. What a firing squad execution looked like through my eyes
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — I've now watched through glass and bars as 11 men were put to death at a South Carolina prison. None of the previous 10 prepared me for watching the firing squad death of Brad Sigmon on Friday night. I might now be unique among U.S.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A South Carolina man executed by firing squad is the first US prisoner killed this way in 15 years
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — A South Carolina man who killed his ex-girlfriend’s parents with a baseball bat was executed by firing squad Friday, the first U.S. prisoner in 15 years to die by that method, which he saw as preferable to the electric chair or lethal injection.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Homeland Security ends TSA collective bargaining agreement, in effort to dismantle union protections
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Department of Homeland Security said Friday that it is ending the collective bargaining agreement with the tens of thousands of frontline employees at the Transportation Security Administration, marking a major effort to dismantle union protections under the Trump administratio
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: CDC launches 'conflicts of interest' page about vaccine advisory panel
item.description: The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) published a page on Friday listing the conflicts of interest reported by members of a key vaccine advisory committee.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, KRON4

item.title: Federal judge won't order immigration officials to change school arrests policy
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Immigration authorities don't need to revert to a Biden-era policy limiting arrests at schools after officials in Denver challenged new policies from the Trump administration, a federal judge ruled Friday. U.S.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Defense Department begins staffing cuts as 31,000 employees offer to resign: report
item.description: A few thousand Department of Defense employees are expected to be terminated as part of the Trump administration's effort to downsize the agency.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump administration throws hundreds of affordable housing projects into limbo after contract cuts
item.description: The Trump administration has stalled at least $60 million in funding intended largely for affordable housing developments nationwide, throwing hundreds of projects into a precarious limbo, according to information and documents obtained by The Associated Press The move is part of a flurry of funding
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: State Dept using AI to ban visas over perceived pro-Hamas posts
item.description: The initiative, started under Secretary of State Marco Rubio, will use AI to scan student visa holders’ social media accounts.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lawsuit aims to strike down LGBTQ antidiscrimination protections in Pennsylvania
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — Two public school districts and several parents have sued Pennsylvania in a bid to undo antidiscrimination protections for gay and transgender people, saying the state's two-year-old regulation is illegal because it goes beyond what lawmakers intended or allowed.
item.dc:creator: MARC LEVY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Louisiana student died after being punched in hazing ritual, police say
item.description: Baton Rouge police confirmed Southern University student Caleb Wilson died after being punched in the chest multiple times during a hazing ritual. One arrest has been made, and more suspects are expected.
item.dc:creator: Keymonte Avery, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tony! Toni! Tone! guitarist D'Wayne Wiggins dies
item.description: D'Wayne Wiggins, guitarist and founding member of Tony! Toni! Tone!, has died, according to a social media post from his family. The guitarist, who was a native of Oakland, had been battling bladder cancer.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Gene Hackman, Betsy Arakawa causes of death announced
item.description: The causes of death for actor Gene Hackman and his wife Betsy Arakawa were released Friday after days of mystery and speculation.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump signs executive order creating bitcoin reserve
item.description: Trump, who once called crypto a "scam," has drastically changed his tune on the subject and promised on the campaign trail to make the U.S. the "crypto capital of the world.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Life-saving avalanche forecasts could be hindered by Trump budget cuts
item.description: LEADVILLE, Colo. (AP) — Officials in the Western U.S. who warn the public about avalanches are sounding a different type of alarm.
item.dc:creator: BRITTANY PETERSON and MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Alarming number of Americans scammed out of life savings have one thing in common, prompting lawmaker response
item.description: With romance scams on the rise, a bipartisan group of lawmakers is introducing new legislation to hold fraudsters accountable for targeting Americans' savings.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Expert tells judge to drop charges against NYC Mayor Eric Adams without letting them be refiled
item.description: A court-appointed lawyer has advised a federal judge to let the Justice Department drop corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams but prevent prosecutors from ever reviving the case so it doesn't hang over him “like the proverbial Sword of Damocles.”.
item.dc:creator: Larry Neumeister And Michael R. Sisak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Judge says plans to release a woman in Slender Man case can go forward
item.description: WAUKESHA, Wis. (AP) — A Wisconsin woman who nearly killed her classmate years ago to please horror character Slender Man can be released from a psychiatric hospital as planned, a judge decided Thursday, rejecting state health officials’ last-minute attempt to keep her committed.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 90s band Spin Doctors' equipment stolen from Pennsylvania storage unit
item.description: Spin Doctors are best known for their 1992 hit singles, "Little Miss Can't Be Wrong" and "Two Princes," from their 1991 album "Pocket Full of Kryptonite.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Muniz, NewsNation

item.title: New Mexico man found guilty of 'decapitating' man, 'playing soccer' with his head
item.description: Arciniega-Saenz, a homeless person at the time of the crime, stabbed Garcia 84 times with a small switchblade, the DA's Office said.
item.dc:creator: Luisa Barrios, NewsNation

item.title: A 40-day Target boycott began this week. What to know about the protest and its potential impact
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A 40-day boycott of Target that calls for supporters to give up shopping at the company's stores during the Lenten period kicked off this week, to protest the discount retailer's decision to end some of its diversity, equity and inclusion initiatives. The Rev.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Cockpit recorder likely hadn't worked 'for several years' before jet crashed in Philadelphia: NTSB
item.description: The cockpit voice recorder in the medical transport jet that slammed into a Philadelphia neighborhood in January had likely not worked for years, according to a preliminary report released by the National Transportation Safety Board (NTSB) Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WFLA, NBC

item.title: President Trump delays some tariffs on Mexico and Canada
item.description: President Donald Trump Thursday paused the 25% tariffs on Canada and Mexico that started earlier this week, with plans to restart them in April.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Biertempfel, KTLA 5

item.title: Ghost preparers and spear phishing: Here's the tax scams to avoid
item.description: With the tax filing deadline coming up on April 15, the IRS released its annual "Dirty Dozen" list of tax scams for 2025 to warn people of common schemes.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: Oregon man accused of throwing Molotov cocktails, shooting at Tesla dealership
item.description: Adam Matthew Lansky, 41, has been arraigned on federal charges after being accused of attempting to destroy property at a Tesla dealership twice.
item.dc:creator: Aimee Plante, Fox 5

item.title: Florida man threatens to assassinate Trump, destroy New York with missiles in 911 calls: report
item.description: Justin Blaxton, 34, allegedly threatened to assassinate President Donald Trump and launch missiles to destroy New York in a series of 911 calls.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House votes to censure Al Green for disrupting Trump speech
item.description: The House voted Thursday to censure Rep. Al Green (D-Texas) for his protest during President Trump’s address to Congress this week — a disruptive display that prompted his removal from the House chamber.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, ABC 4

item.title: 3 skiers missing after avalanche hits remote mountain range in Alaska
item.description: Alaska State Troopers said three skiers are likely dead after an avalanche buried them in the Chugach Mountains near Girdwood, Alaska, on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New Google feature flags when you might be getting scammed in real time
item.description: Google is rolling out new, AI-powered tools to help stop scammers in their tracks, before they get your data or money.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, ABC 4

item.title: Trump's plans to slash IRS staff could derail agency amid tax season
item.description: It would likely contribute to a longer-term trend at the agency of diminished audit rates, minimal tax enforcement for the most sophisticated taxpayers, and the use of outdated technologies that’s been going on for decades.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, Fox 5

item.title: Trump administration deletes list of hundreds of federal buildings targeted for potential sale
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Trump administration on Tuesday published a list of more than 440 federal properties it had identified to potentially offload, including the FBI headquarters and the main Department of Justice building, after deeming them “not core to government operations.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, MICHAEL R. SISAK and JOSHUA GOODMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Two men charged after Kansas City Chiefs fans found in backyard
item.description: Two men have been charged in connection to the deaths of three Kansas City Chiefs fans who were found dead in the backyard of a home in January 2024.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Johnson, Fox 5

item.title: Baja Midnight, Quesocrisp Taco, and more: What’s coming to Taco Bell in 2025
item.description: Taco Bell announced a slate of new menu items and plans for the fast food chain at its second annual Live Mas event, which took place in New York City on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump wants to use the 'God Squad' to increase logging, but it must follow strict rules
item.description: President Donald Trump wants to increase logging in national forests and on public lands, including by bypassing endangered species protections.
item.dc:creator: TAMMY WEBBER, Associated Press, ABC 4
